ground_truth.title: Sharing Secrets With Lawmakers: Congress as a User of Intelligence... CS1 97-10001... Center for the Study Intelligence... 02/97

cab.declassify_on: NA to AEA portions, see source list for NSI portions

paragraphs.text: G ROUND RULES ON INTELLIGENCE-SHARING WERE AGREED TO IN THE 1970S, BUT NONE WERE WRITTEN DOWN. AND OVER TIME THESE UNDERSTAND-INGS OFTEN GAVE WAY IN THE CONTINUING TUSSLE BETWEEN THE OVERSEERS AND THE OVERSEEN. TWENTY YEARS LATER, THE SYSTEM STILL OPERATES WITHOUT FORMAL RULES " OF THE ROAD.

paragraphs.mark: //JOINT TS AUS CAN ITA USA//REL TO USA, AUS, CAN, ITA, EU
paragraphs.text: Sharing Secrets With Lawmakers: Congress as a User of Intelligence
This publication is prepared for the use of US Government officials, and the format, coverage, and content are designed to meet their specific requirements
Requesters outside the US Government may obtain subscriptions to publications similar to this one by addressing inquiries to

paragraphs.text: Comments and queries on this publication may be directed to the DOCEX Project at the above address or by phone (202-707-9527) or fax (202-707-0380), or to the NTIS Office of Customer Services at the above address or by phone (703-487-4660). This study will be available on the Internet after 20 March 1997 at www.odci.gov/csi
